title: CS2 Resolution & Aspect Ratio Calculator — Stretched vs Black Bars | CSDB.gg
description: Calculate the best CS2 resolution for your monitor. Compare 4:3 stretched, 16:9, and 16:10 aspect ratios. See FOV differences, pros and cons, and what the pros use.
heading: CS2 Resolution & Aspect Ratio Calculator
intro: Find the perfect resolution for your CS2 setup. Compare aspect ratios, understand FOV differences, and see what resolution the pros use.

faqs.question: Is 4:3 stretched better than 16:9 in CS2?
faqs.answer: 4:3 stretched makes player models appear wider, which some players find easier to aim at. However, you lose peripheral vision (reduced horizontal FOV). Most pros use 4:3 stretched, but it is personal preference — 16:9 gives you more information on screen.

faqs.question: What resolution do most CS2 pros use?
faqs.answer: The most popular pro resolution is 1280x960 (4:3 stretched), followed by 1024x768 and 1920x1080 (native 16:9). About 60% of professional players use some form of 4:3 resolution.

faqs.question: Does resolution affect FPS in CS2?
faqs.answer: Yes. Lower resolutions render fewer pixels, which can significantly boost FPS. Switching from 1920x1080 to 1280x960 can increase FPS by 30-50% depending on your GPU. This is why many players on older hardware prefer lower resolutions.

faqs.question: What is the difference between stretched and black bars?
faqs.answer: Both use a 4:3 resolution. Stretched fills your entire screen by stretching the image horizontally — models look wider but distorted. Black bars keep the native aspect ratio with unused black space on the sides — models look normal but your usable screen area is smaller.

faqs.question: How do I set up stretched resolution in CS2?
faqs.answer: Set your in-game resolution to a 4:3 option (like 1280x960), then in your GPU control panel (NVIDIA Control Panel or AMD Radeon Settings) set the display scaling mode to "Full-screen" instead of "Aspect ratio". This forces the image to stretch across your entire monitor.
